category_title: Timid students who feel voiceless and isolated in peer work
minimal_context: Brandon is standing in front of his 6th-grade science class to deliver a lesson as a co-teacher.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Brandon, please stand in front of the class and deliver the science lesson from the lesson plan.

STUDENT: (Stands in front of the class full of his peers) Okay...

TUTOR: Go ahead and teach them the science content.

STUDENT: (Talking slow) Today... we are... going to...

TUTOR: Keep trying.

STUDENT: (Very timid) I'm really nervous speaking in front of a class full of my peers. I'm talking slow... I usually feel like I have no voice because my partner doesn't like to talk to me.

options.A: The tutor asks the student questions directly about the science content, but also allows him the time to give his response in front of his peers.
options.B: The tutor pays close attention to the science lesson being delivered and guides the instruction (by raising a hand as a traditional student would) only when there are content issues.
options.C: The tutor stays calm and confident when dealing with control issues while the student is standing in front of the class to deliver the science lesson.
options.D: The tutor allows the student to withdraw from the high-stimulation classroom environment for a time-limited period (Quiet Time) to prevent an emotional overload during the lesson.
